---
title: "What Drives Firms to Go Green?"
subtitle: "Cross-Country Evidence from Enterprise Surveys"
author: "Sophiko Skhirtladze"
institute: "University of Chicago, Harris School of Public Policy"
date: "3/5/2026"
format:
  beamer:
    theme: metropolis
    colortheme: default
    fonttheme: structurebold
    aspectratio: 169
    fontsize: 9pt
    toc: false
    slide-level: 2
    header-includes: |
      \usepackage{booktabs}
      \usepackage{graphicx}
      \usepackage{xcolor}
      \usepackage{tikz}
      \definecolor{harrisorange}{HTML}{D14124}
      \definecolor{harrisblue}{HTML}{1A3E5E}
      \definecolor{lightgray}{HTML}{F5F5F5}
      \setbeamercolor{frametitle}{bg=harrisblue, fg=white}
      \setbeamercolor{title separator}{fg=harrisorange}
      \setbeamercolor{progress bar}{fg=harrisorange}
      \setbeamercolor{alerted text}{fg=harrisorange}
      \setbeamerfont{title}{size=\large, series=\bfseries}
      \setbeamerfont{subtitle}{size=\small}
      \setbeamerfont{frametitle}{size=\normalsize, series=\bfseries}
      \setbeamertemplate{footline}{
        \leavevmode
        \hbox{
          \begin{beamercolorbox}[wd=.5\paperwidth,ht=2.5ex,dp=1ex,left,leftskip=1em]{author in head/foot}
            \usebeamerfont{author in head/foot}\insertshortauthor
          \end{beamercolorbox}
          \begin{beamercolorbox}[wd=.5\paperwidth,ht=2.5ex,dp=1ex,right,rightskip=1em]{title in head/foot}
            \usebeamerfont{title in head/foot}\insertframenumber\,/\,\inserttotalframenumber
          \end{beamercolorbox}
        }
        \vskip0pt
      }
---

## Research Question, Data \& Methodology

\begin{columns}[T]

\begin{column}{0.32\textwidth}
\textcolor{harrisblue}{\textbf{Research Question}}
\vspace{0.15cm}

\footnotesize What drives firms in developed and developing countries to adopt green practices — and how much do \alert{firm capabilities} vs. \alert{national policy environments} matter?

\vspace{0.2cm}

\textbf{Two outcomes:}
\begin{itemize}
  \footnotesize
  \item CO\textsubscript{2} emissions monitoring
  \item Energy management adoption
\end{itemize}

\end{column}

\begin{column}{0.01\textwidth}
\textcolor{lightgray}{\rule{0.5pt}{0.75\textheight}}
\end{column}

\begin{column}{0.31\textwidth}
\textcolor{harrisblue}{\textbf{Data Sources}}
\vspace{0.15cm}

\footnotesize
\begin{itemize}
  \item \textbf{World Bank Enterprise Survey(WBES)} (2022–2025): 70k+ firms, 100 countries
  \item \textbf{Environmental Performance Index (EPI)}  2024: 58 indicators, 180 countries
  \item \textbf{World Bank Business Ready (B-READY)} 2024: environmental regulatory index, 50 countries
  \item \textbf{World Governance Indicators (WGI)}: 6 governance dimensions
  \item \textbf{World Development Indicators (WDI)}: macro controls (GDP, trade, credit)
\end{itemize}

\end{column}

\begin{column}{0.01\textwidth}
\textcolor{lightgray}{\rule{0.5pt}{0.75\textheight}}
\end{column}

\begin{column}{0.31\textwidth}
\textcolor{harrisblue}{\textbf{Methodology}}
\vspace{0.15cm}

\footnotesize
\textbf{Step 1 — Baseline logit} with country \& year fixed effects: identifies firm-level correlates within countries

\vspace{0.15cm}

\textbf{Step 2 — Multilevel random-intercept logit}: adds country-level covariates in stages:
\begin{enumerate}
  \footnotesize
  \item Macro controls
  \item + Governance (WGI)
  \item + EPI
  \item + B-READY index
\end{enumerate}

\vspace{0.15cm}
\tiny \textit{Clustered SEs at country level. glmer via lme4.}

\end{column}

\end{columns}

---

## Descriptive Analysis: Income \& Sector Heterogeneity

\begin{center}
  \includegraphics[width=0.95\textwidth,height=0.80\textheight,keepaspectratio]{../data/derived-data/adoption_heatmap.pdf}
\end{center}


---

## Econometric Analysis: Marginal Effects --- CO\textsubscript{2} Monitoring

\begin{center}
  \includegraphics[width=0.97\textwidth,height=0.82\textheight,keepaspectratio]{../data/derived-data/marginal_effects_ctrl_co.pdf}
\end{center}
\vspace{0.05cm}
\begin{center}
\tiny \textcolor{gray}{$^{*}p < 0.1$ \quad $^{**}p < 0.05$ \quad $^{***}p < 0.01$ \quad (average marginal effects at means, clustered SE)}
\end{center}

---

## Econometric Analysis: Marginal Effects --- Energy Management

\begin{center}
  \includegraphics[width=0.97\textwidth,height=0.82\textheight,keepaspectratio]{../data/derived-data/marginal_effects_ctrl_em.pdf}
\end{center}
\vspace{0.05cm}
\begin{center}
\tiny \textcolor{gray}{$^{*}p < 0.1$ \quad $^{**}p < 0.05$ \quad $^{***}p < 0.01$ \quad (average marginal effects at means, clustered SE)}
\end{center}

---

## Deep Dive: EPI Matters, But Asymmetrically

\begin{columns}[T]

\begin{column}{0.48\textwidth}
\centering
\includegraphics[width=\textwidth,height=0.75\textheight,keepaspectratio]{../data/derived-data/epi_vs_country_fe_co.pdf}
\vspace{0.03cm}

\tiny \textcolor{harrisblue}{CO\textsubscript{2} Monitoring · r = +0.03}
\end{column}

\begin{column}{0.48\textwidth}
\centering
\includegraphics[width=\textwidth,height=0.75\textheight,keepaspectratio]{../data/derived-data/epi_vs_country_fe_em.pdf}
\vspace{0.03cm}

\tiny \textcolor{harrisorange}{Energy Management · r = +0.19}
\end{column}

\end{columns}

---

## Policy Implications \& Takeaways

\vspace{0.15cm}

\begin{columns}[T]

\begin{column}{0.31\textwidth}
\begin{tikzpicture}
  \fill[harrisblue!10] (0,0) rectangle (3.8,4.5);
  \draw[harrisblue, thick] (0,4.5) -- (3.8,4.5);
\end{tikzpicture}

\vspace{-4.0cm}
\hspace{0.1cm}
\begin{minipage}{3.6cm}
  \textcolor{harrisblue}{\textbf{Build Firm Capabilities}}
  \vspace{0.1cm}

  \footnotesize Training, R\&D support, quality infrastructure, and financial access are prerequisites for green adoption.

  \vspace{0.15cm}
  \tiny \textit{Policy multiplier: capable firms better absorb regulatory compliance costs and new technologies.}
\end{minipage}
\end{column}

\begin{column}{0.31\textwidth}
\begin{tikzpicture}
  \fill[harrisorange!10] (0,0) rectangle (3.8,4.5);
  \draw[harrisorange, thick] (0,4.5) -- (3.8,4.5);
\end{tikzpicture}

\vspace{-4.0cm}
\hspace{0.1cm}
\begin{minipage}{3.6cm}
  \textcolor{harrisorange}{\textbf{Leverage Procurement}}
  \vspace{0.1cm}

  \footnotesize Government contracts are the most robust institutional predictor. Embedding environmental criteria in procurement can green firms \textit{without} new standalone regulation.

  \vspace{0.15cm}
  \tiny \textit{Strong effect on energy management suggests operational, not just reporting, improvements.}
\end{minipage}
\end{column}

\begin{column}{0.31\textwidth}
\begin{tikzpicture}
  \fill[harrisblue!10] (0,0) rectangle (3.8,4.5);
  \draw[harrisblue, thick] (0,4.5) -- (3.8,4.5);
\end{tikzpicture}

\vspace{-4.0cm}
\hspace{0.1cm}
\begin{minipage}{3.6cm}
  \textcolor{harrisblue}{\textbf{Prioritize Enforcement over Design}}
  \vspace{0.1cm}

  \footnotesize EPI (realized performance) predicts adoption; B-READY (regulatory design) does not.

  \vspace{0.15cm}
  \tiny \textit{The existence of environmental provisions in business regulation is insufficient without credible implementation.}
\end{minipage}
\end{column}

\end{columns}
